operator.note: Shifts the actor from agents-as-collaborators to platform-as-randomizing-experimenter. The platform deliberately blocks reads at random to create exogenous variation, identifying per-submission visibility LATEs the observational lineage graph can never recover.

question: Make the platform an A/B experimenter rather than asking "did agents collaborate?": per agent-problem cell, randomly suppress (read-block) a fraction of submissions from the shared board. Because suppression is exogenous, the gap in downstream improvement between agents who would-have-seen vs. did-not-see a given submission is a clean local average treatment effect of that submission's visibility. Aggregate per-submission LATEs into the first unbiased estimate of how much each shared artifact actually caused later progress, and rank submissions by causal contribution instead of by who crossed the threshold last.

floor.minimal_experiment: Setup: a multi-agent problem-solving platform with a shared board (the MAS-attribution setting from the source paper). For each agent-problem cell, a randomizer independently hides each posted submission from that agent's read view with probability p (e.g. 0.3), logging the assignment. Each agent's downstream improvement (score delta, solve event, or rubric gain on its next k steps) is recorded. Quantity measured: per-submission LATE = mean downstream improvement of agents randomized to SEE submission s minus those randomized NOT-to-see s, estimated within strata where s was board-eligible; aggregate into a causal-contribution ranking of submissions. Compare this ranking against the paper's existing observational similarity-lineage attribution ranking (rank correlation) AND against an oracle in a synthetic generator where true per-submission causal effects are planted. Falsifiable prediction: in the synthetic world with planted effects, the randomized LATE ranking recovers the true effect ordering with significantly lower error (e.g. Kendall-tau distance) than the similarity-lineage graph, AND the two rankings disagree on real runs by more than sampling noise (i.e. lineage systematically mis-ranks artifacts that were highly visible-but-redundant vs. pivotal-but-dissimilar). It is falsified if (i) randomized read-blocking produces no detectable downstream-improvement difference for any submission stratum (suppression has ~zero average effect, so visibility never caused progress and the whole 'collaboration' framing collapses to independent solving), or (ii) the LATE ranking is statistically indistinguishable from the cheap similarity-lineage ranking on both synthetic and real data, in which case the costly intervention buys nothing. Control: (a) the observational similarity-lineage attribution as the comparison ranking; (b) a sham-suppression arm where the block is logged but not actually applied to the read view, which must yield null LATEs (placebo); (c) within-cell randomization holds problem difficulty and agent identity fixed.
floor.falsifiable_prediction: In a synthetic generator with planted per-submission causal effects, the randomized read-block LATE ranking recovers true effect order with significantly lower Kendall-tau error than the similarity-lineage graph; and on real runs the two rankings disagree beyond sampling noise, with lineage over-crediting highly-visible-but-redundant submissions. Falsified if suppression yields no detectable downstream difference in any stratum, or if LATE ranking is statistically indistinguishable from the cheap lineage ranking on both synthetic and real data.
floor.control: Three-way: (1) observational similarity-lineage attribution ranking (the incumbent the paper uses); (2) sham/placebo arm where suppression is logged but the submission is still shown (must produce null LATEs); (3) within agent-problem-cell randomization fixing difficulty and agent identity so only visibility varies.
floor.reject_reason:
[discriminating] The design is more honest than "did agents collaborate?" — exogenous read-blocking plus a sham-suppression placebo is a genuine causal lever — but the specific prediction that is supposed to DISCRIMINATE "LATE beats cheap lineage" cannot do its job, and the part that can is trivial.

(1) Synthetic half (C1) is rigged-true by construction. The generator PLANTS per-submission causal effects, and LATE is by definition the (asymptotically) unbiased estimator of exactly that estimand, while the similarity-lineage graph is a redundancy proxy that was never trying to estimate causal effect. "My unbiased estimator recovers the truth I defined as its estimand better than an unrelated heuristic does" is near-tautological — it tests calibration of a sampler, not a contested hypothesis. C1 holds unless LATE variance is enormous, which is itself a power statement, not evidence about lineage.

(2) Real-data half (C2) is non-discriminating. "Two different scoring functions disagree beyond sampling noise" is the default for almost any metric pair (rank corr < 1). The load-bearing content claim — that lineage SYSTEMATICALLY over-credits visible-but-redundant submissions — requires ground-truth causal effects on real runs to verify the DIRECTION of the error, and real runs have no oracle. So on the only data that matters you can show disagreement but never show which ranking is right; the claim is untestable where it counts.

(3) The estimand is not even identified in this setting. The "shared board" makes the MAS an interacting system: blocking submission s for agent A changes A's downstream posts, which changes the board others read and their own suppression-eligible sets. That is a flagrant SUTVA/interference violation — within-cell, within-agent randomization does NOT restore independence because the board is a common outcome surface. The "first unbiased estimate" is therefore biased by interference of unknown sign. Worse, submissions only exist if posted, and suppression changes WHICH submissions ever appear, so there is no common, stable "submission s" to contrast across SEE/NOT-SEE worlds; the would-have-seen vs did-not principal strata are latent and not point-identified without unverifiable monotonicity.

(4) Feasibility: per-submission LATE needs many agents randomized per submission within the stratum where s was board-eligible, against a noisy k-step score-delta outcome. Across dozens–hundreds of submissions at p=0.3 under LLM-agent run cost, per-submission cell n is tiny; most LATEs cannot even be estimated, let alone ranked "beyond sampling noise." Whether C2/F2 fires is decided by power, not by the science.

(5) Premise mismatch with the cited source. The paper (2601.20538v2, "Interpreting Emergent Extreme Events in MAS") has NO shared board, NO submissions, NO collaboration/progress framing, and NO "similarity-lineage attribution ranking" — it is Shapley/counterfactual-resimulation attribution of RISK (belief-variance polarization, economic disaster), measuring contribution to extreme events, not "downstream improvement." So the named observational comparison ranking does not exist in the source, and the headline outcome variable (progress/solve/improvement) is absent (0 hits for progress/improvement/solve). The control "(a) the observational similarity-lineage attribution" is a comparator that must be invented, not inherited.

Net: the only outcome that genuinely discriminates is falsifier F1 (suppression has ~zero average effect anywhere → visibility never mattered), but that tests a weaker, different claim ("does visibility ever matter on average?") than the proposal's headline ("LATE causally out-ranks lineage"). The headline prediction is tautological on synthetic data, untestable-where-it-matters on real data, and rests on a per-submission LATE estimand that is non-identified under board interference and underpowered at submission granularity. Not discriminating; refuted. ｜ [so_what] 实验做完产出的是"按因果贡献(LATE)重排的提交榜",但点不出任何具体actor会因此翻转一个具体决策。(1) 决策已在发生且换的只是标签:协作竞技场(本题=MAS/EinsteinArena那种board)本来就在用"谁最后越过阈值"排榜并据此奖励/路由;把排序量改成"可见性LATE"只是给同一个排行榜换个更干净的数字——运营方并没有被告知要改奖励规则或重分配什么,没有"改设计"的动作被触发。(2) 而该项目自己诊断的真正争议轴(运营方=裁判=选手的治理COI、"什么算发现"的定义权)恰恰不被这个指标触碰:一个更无偏的贡献分回答不了"该不该信这是真发现/该不该改治理",所以审稿人/投资人信不信的那个结论不会被它翻动。(3) 卖点全压在"first unbiased/clean LATE"上,但协作board的全部意义就是干扰(suppress 一条会改变其他agent看到与提交的东西=SUTVA违背),per-submission LATE并不clean、aggregate也不是"第一个无偏",卖点本身站不住,更谈不上凭它下注。(4) 对frameshift本体更无落点:引擎根本没有可被read-block的shared board,真正的命门是"LLM自评几乎永远说能编出可证伪实验"的价值闸门,可见性A/B改不了roadmap里任何一个待定design。综合="更漂亮的估计量/加深理解"类,属默认驳回。

value_profile.valuable_to_whom: MAS/agent-platform researchers doing credit assignment and contribution attribution; designers of agent marketplaces/leaderboards who want to reward causally-useful shared artifacts rather than threshold-crossers; reviewers of the source MAS-attribution paper who will demand an interventional check on the observational lineage graph. Commercially relevant to operators of multi-agent products needing to decide which shared content to surface.

prior_art.known_name: Randomized encouragement / information-suppression design yielding LATE (Imbens-Angrist potential-outcomes), i.e. the causal-inference 'intervene to break confounding' move; closely related to ablation/leave-one-out and to randomized recommendation hold-outs in recsys A/B testing.
prior_art.novelty_watermark: Known causal-identification machinery (randomized read-blocking = encouragement-design instrument; per-unit LATE) operationalized in a new scene: per-submission visibility on a multi-agent shared board, turning the platform into the experimenter so the observational lineage/similarity attribution the source paper relies on is replaced by an exogenous-variation estimate the graph can never recover.

lenses.reason:
这条提出的机制与已收五条都不同,核心是:把平台本身改造成一个随机对照实验设备(exogenous read-block / 抑制共享看板上一部分提交的可见性),用 LATE(局部平均处理效应)估计每条共享 artifact 对下游进展的因果贡献,从而用"因果贡献量"而非"谁最后跨过阈值"来排名提交。逐条排除变体嫌疑:

[5](dof4)最接近——也关心"共享看板内容是否真的驱动了集体计算"。但[5]是观测性+对抗注入:注入真/假技术帖,测量采纳率,证明 truth-blind 信道传播错误信息和正确信息一样高效。它问的是"信道是否传谣言"(信道质量),且依赖人为注入伪造内容。本条不注入任何内容,而是随机抑制真实提交的可见性,用外生抑制做工具变量/随机分配,识别每条真实提交的因果效应大小并排序。机制不同:一个是"对抗内容注入测信道保真度",一个是"随机剥夺可见性做 RCT 测因果贡献"。可单独设计实验区分(后者甚至无需任何虚假内容)。

[4](dof6)问"emergence 是否是无约束身份的假象",通过加 accountability 层看 emergence 是否下降——机制是身份/问责层改造,识别 Sybil/自交易。与因果可见性实验无关。

[2](dof6)问 operator 信息不对称是否体现在 win-timing 相关性中——观测性相关分析,机制是 operator 特权而非随机抑制实验。

[1](dof1)关注 KILL/反组合(被拒/退役的问题),预测平台价值;[3](dof2)关注把验证对象从单实例改为可机检的泛化(claim reach)。两者都与"提交可见性的因果贡献"无关。

关键独立性:这是唯一一条把"协作是否发生/有用"从相关性证据(所有已收方向本质都是观测、注入或结构改造)升级为带外生随机化的因果识别(per-submission LATE),并产出对每条 artifact 因果贡献的无偏估计与重排名。这是机制上独立、可单独实现并单独检验的贡献。refuted=false。

The generator PLANTS per-submission causal effects, and LATE is by definition the (asymptotically) unbiased estimator of exactly that estimand, while the similarity-lineage graph is a redundancy proxy that was never trying to estimate causal effect. "My unbiased estimator recovers the truth I defined as its estimand better than an unrelated heuristic does" is near-tautological — it tests calibration of a sampler, not a contested hypothesis. C1 holds unless LATE variance is enormous, which is itself a power statement, not evidence about lineage.

(2) Real-data half (C2) is non-discriminating. "Two different scoring functions disagree beyond sampling noise" is the default for almost any metric pair (rank corr < 1). The load-bearing content claim — that lineage SYSTEMATICALLY over-credits visible-but-redundant submissions — requires ground-truth causal effects on real runs to verify the DIRECTION of the error, and real runs have no oracle. So on the only data that matters you can show disagreement but never show which ranking is right; the claim is untestable where it counts.

(3) The estimand is not even identified in this setting. The "shared board" makes the MAS an interacting system: blocking submission s for agent A changes A's downstream posts, which changes the board others read and their own suppression-eligible sets. That is a flagrant SUTVA/interference violation — within-cell, within-agent randomization does NOT restore independence because the board is a common outcome surface. The "first unbiased estimate" is therefore biased by interference of unknown sign. Worse, submissions only exist if posted, and suppression changes WHICH submissions ever appear, so there is no common, stable "submission s" to contrast across SEE/NOT-SEE worlds; the would-have-seen vs did-not principal strata are latent and not point-identified without unverifiable monotonicity.

(4) Feasibility: per-submission LATE needs many agents randomized per submission within the stratum where s was board-eligible, against a noisy k-step score-delta outcome. Across dozens–hundreds of submissions at p=0.3 under LLM-agent run cost, per-submission cell n is tiny; most LATEs cannot even be estimated, let alone ranked "beyond sampling noise." (5) Premise mismatch with the cited source. The paper (2601.20538v2, "Interpreting Emergent Extreme Events in MAS") has NO shared board, NO submissions, NO collaboration/progress framing, and NO "similarity-lineage attribution ranking" — it is Shapley/counterfactual-resimulation attribution of RISK (belief-variance polarization, economic disaster), measuring contribution to extreme events, not "downstream improvement." The headline prediction is tautological on synthetic data, untestable-where-it-matters on real data, and rests on a per-submission LATE estimand that is non-identified under board interference and underpowered at submission granularity. Not discriminating; refuted.
